{{Short description|2021 film award ceremony}} {{pp-sock|small=yes}} {{Use British English|date=April 2021}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2021}} {{Infobox film awards | award = British Academy Film Awards | number = 74 | date = 10–11 April 2021 | image = | site = [[Royal Albert Hall]], [[London]] | host = [[Clara Amfo]] (Opening Night)
[[Edith Bowman]] and [[Dermot O'Leary]] (Main ceremony) | best_film = ''[[Nomadland]]'' | best_british = ''[[Promising Young Woman]]'' | best_actor = [[Anthony Hopkins]] | best_actor_film = ''[[The Father (2020 film)|The Father]]'' | best_actress = [[Frances McDormand]] | best_actress_film = ''[[Nomadland]]'' | most_wins = ''[[Nomadland]]'' (4) | most_nominations = ''[[Nomadland]]'' and ''[[Rocks (film)|Rocks]]'' (7) | last = 73rd | next = [[75th British Academy Film Awards|75th]] }} The '''74th British Academy Film Awards''', also known as the BAFTAs, were held on 10 and 11 April 2021 at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2020 in film|2020]] and early [[2021 in film|2021]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2020 and early 2021.{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2020/film/global/bafta-moves-film-awards-to-april-11-1234635754/|title=BAFTA Moves 2021 Film Awards to April 11, Two Weeks Before the Oscars|last=Ravindran|first=Manori|date=15 January 2021|work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|access-date=18 January 2021}}{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2021/01/bafta-voting-opens-top-brass-diversity-tweaks-pandemic-impact-1234671807/|title=BAFTA Voting Opens: Top Brass Discuss Diversity Tweaks & Pandemic Impact|last=Grater|first=Tom|date=12 January 2021|work=[[Deadline Hollywood]]|access-date=18 January 2021}} The nominees were announced on 9 March 2021.{{cite web|url=https://www.bafta.org/film/awards/2021-nominations-winners|title=2021 EE British Academy Film Awards: Nominations and winners|publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]]|access-date=9 March 2021}} The American drama ''[[Nomadland]]'' and British [[coming-of-age story|coming-of-age]] drama ''[[Rocks (film)|Rocks]]'' received the most nominations with seven each; the former ultimately won four, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]].{{cite web|url=https://apnews.com/article/hugh-grant-tom-hiddleston-priyanka-chopra-coronavirus-pandemic-chloe-zhao-fd6ce441111615517945a5fed6358336|title='Nomadland' wins 4 BAFTAs including best picture, director|last=Lawless|first=Jill|date=11 April 2021|publisher=[[Associated Press]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} ==Winners and nominees== [[File:Chloe Zhao by Gage Skidmore.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Chloé Zhao]], Best Director winner and Best Film co-winner]] [[File:Anthony Hopkins-Tuscan Sun Festival.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Anthony Hopkins]], Best Actor winner]] [[File:Frances McDormand 2015 (cropped).jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Frances McDormand]], Best Actress winner and Best Film co-winner]] [[File:Daniel Kaluuya by Gage Skidmore.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Daniel Kaluuya]], Best Supporting Actor winner]] [[File:Yoon Yeo-jeong at the 2016 Women In Film Korea Festival and Kim Taer-ri at the 2016 Women In Film Korea Festival.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Youn Yuh-jung]], Best Supporting Actress winner]] [[File:Emerald Fennell, 2023 (1).jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Emerald Fennell]], Best Original Screenplay winner and Outstanding British Film co-winner]] [[File:Hampton3.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Christopher Hampton]], Best Adapted Screenplay co-winner]] [[File:Florian Zeller.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Florian Zeller]], Best Adapted Screenplay co-winner]] [[File:Thomas Vinterberg Berlinale 2010.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Thomas Vinterberg]], Best Film Not in the English Language co-winner]] [[File:Jon Batiste 2018 (cropped).jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Jon Batiste]], Best Original Music co-winner]] [[File:Reznor Ross G5 setup cropped tight.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Trent Reznor]] (left) and [[Atticus Ross]] (right), Best Original Music co-winners]] The nominees were announced on 9 March 2021. The winners were announced on 10 and 11 April 2021.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-56323950|title=Bafta Film Awards 2021: The winners and nominees in full|date=11 April 2021|publisher=[[BBC News]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} ===BAFTA Fellowship=== {{Main|BAFTA Fellowship}} * [[Ang Lee]]{{cite web|url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/ang-lee-to-be-honoured-with-bafta-fellowship-2021|title=Ground-breaking contemporary filmmaker Ang Lee to be honoured with BAFTA Fellowship|date=6 April 2021|publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]]|access-date=6 April 2021}} ===Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema=== {{Main|BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award}} * [[Noel Clarke]]{{cite web|url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-outstanding-contribution-to-cinema-2021|title=Noel Clarke to be honoured at the 2021 EE British Academy Film Awards for Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema|date=29 March 2021|publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]]|access-date=29 March 2021|archive-date=29 March 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210329113005/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-outstanding-contribution-to-cinema-2021|url-status=dead}}{{efn|On 29 April 2021, BAFTA revoked Clarke's award and membership of the organisation, following multiple allegations of professional and [[sexual misconduct]]. Many of the allegations came out as a response to the announcement that Clarke would be honoured.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/culture/2021/apr/29/actor-noel-clarke-accused-of-groping-harassment-and-bullying-by-20-women|title='Sexual predator': actor Noel Clarke accused of groping, harassment and bullying by 20 women|last1=Kale|last2=Osborne|first1=Sirin|first2=Lucy|date=29 April 2021|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=29 April 2021}}}} ===Awards=== Winners are listed first, and highlighted in boldface. {| class="wikitable" |- | valign="top" width="50%" | {{Award category|{{BAFTA Film Awards/colour}}|[[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]]}} * '''''[[Nomadland]]'' – [[Mollye Asher]], Dan Janvey, [[Frances McDormand]], [[Peter Spears]] and [[Chloé Zhao]]''' ** ''[[The Father (2020 film)|The Father]]'' – [[Philippe Carcassonne]], [[Jean-Louis Livi]] and [[David Parfitt]] ** ''[[The Mauritanian]]'' – Adam Ackland, Leah Clarke, Beatriz Levin and [[Lloyd Levin]] ** ''[[Promising Young Woman]]'' – Ben Browning, [[Emerald Fennell]], Ashley Fox and Josey McNamara ** ''[[The Trial of the Chicago 7]]'' – [[Stuart M. Award]]}} * '''[[Bukky Bakray]]''' ** [[Conrad Khan]] ** [[Kingsley Ben-Adir]] ** [[Morfydd Clark]] ** [[Sope Dirisu|Ṣọpẹ Dìrísù]] |} ==Ceremony information== Both ceremonies were delayed two months due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]], taking place largely virtually, still lining up with the [[93rd Academy Awards]] set to take place on 25 April 2021, two weeks following both [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]]' (BAFTA) ceremonies.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-56711344|title=Bafta Film Awards 2021: Nomadland and Promising Young Woman win big|date=11 April 2021|publisher=[[BBC News]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} Despite its minimal live elements, the ceremonies still featured a [[red carpet]] in [[London]] and a [[step and repeat]] in [[Los Angeles]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/gallery/2021/apr/11/baftas-2021-the-winners-the-dresses-the-ppe-in-pictures|title=Baftas 2021: the winners, the dresses, the PPE – in pictures|date=11 April 2021|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} The nominees were significantly more diverse than at any previous BAFTA Awards ceremony. Sixteen of the twenty-four nominees in the acting categories were from [[Minority group#Ethnic minorities|ethnic minority]] backgrounds. Four nominees for [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] were women and three of the directors of films nominated for [[BAFTA Award for Best Film Not in the English Language|Best Film Not in the English Language]] were also women.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-56319617|title=Bafta Film Awards 2021: Hugely diverse nominations list unveiled|date=9 March 2021|publisher=[[BBC News]]|access-date=9 March 2021}}{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2021/mar/09/four-women-up-for-best-director-in-strikingly-diverse-bafta-nominations|title=Four women up for best director in strikingly diverse Bafta nominations|last=Shoard|first=Catherine|date=9 March 2021|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=9 March 2021}} The ceremony also introduced a new voting system for nominations after criticism over lack of [[diversity (politics)|diversity]] at the [[73rd British Academy Film Awards]]. In the first round, voters compile a longlist in all categories (with a gender quota in the directing category). It is now compulsory for all voters to watch all long-listed films before the second round. In the second round, the nominees in the directing category and all four acting categories were decided by a small jury.{{cite web|url=https://www.indiewire.com/2020/09/bafta-new-inclusion-guidelines-awards-voting-for-awards-voting-1234588540/|title=BAFTA Reveals New Inclusion Guidelines for Awards Voting|last=Thompson|first=Anne|author-link=Anne Thompson (film journalist)|date=24 September 2020|publisher=[[IndieWire]]|access-date=24 September 2020}}{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/how-new-voting-procedures-created-a-baftas-diversity-surge-in-one-year|title=How New Voting Procedures Created a BAFTAs Diversity Surge in One Year|last=Ritman|first=Alex|date=8 April 2021|work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]|access-date=8 April 2021}} [[BBC]] radio and television presenter [[Clara Amfo]] hosted the Opening Night ceremony from the [[Royal Albert Hall]], which aired on 10 April 2021 on [[BBC Two]] and [[BBC Two#BBC Two HD|BBC Two HD]],{{cite web|url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2021-film-awards-hosts-announced|title=Hosts announced for EE British Academy Film Awards Weekend|date=24 March 2021|publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]]|access-date=24 March 2021}} and was joined by actress and screenwriter [[Joanna Scanlan]], and film critic Rhianna Dhillon.{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2021/film/global/bafta-film-awards-2021-night-one-full-winners-list-1234947765/|title=BAFTA Film Awards 2021: 'Ma Rainey's Black Bottom' Picks Up Two Awards on Opening Night|last=Thorne|first=Will|date=10 April 2021|work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|access-date=11 April 2021}} The first ceremony awarded casting, craft, and short film categories, and [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|Best Actor in a Supporting Role]] nominee [[Leslie Odom Jr.]] performed the song "Speak Now" from the film ''[[One Night in Miami...]]''.{{cite web|url=https://www.standard.co.uk/news/uk/bafta-film-awards-2021-philip-difficult-times-b929027.html|title=Bafta Film Awards 2021: First night host Clara Amfo hails Philip for guiding academy through 'difficult times'|last=Edmonds|first=Lizzie|date=10 April 2021|work=[[Evening Standard]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} [[Edith Bowman]] and [[Dermot O'Leary]] hosted the second night of the ceremony, which aired on 11 April 2021 on [[BBC One]] and [[BBC One#BBC One HD|BBC One HD]]. The co-hosts, who for several years have hosted BAFTA's live red carpet show, were joined by a small group of awards presenters at the Royal Albert Hall, as well as additional presenters via the internet from Los Angeles. At the 11 April ceremony, the Royal Albert Hall was drenched in red light. Bowman and O'Leary led presentations to the all-virtual nominees, alongside a handful of actors as in-person presenters. The first musical performance of the night was a virtual duet, with two holograms of [[Liam Payne]] singing together. Catherine Shoard of ''[[The Guardian]]'' described the live virtual audience, present to laugh and applaud on cue, as "eerie".{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2021/apr/11/baftas-2021-nomadland-wins-big-as-anthony-hopkins-and-promising-young-woman-surprise|title=Baftas 2021: Nomadland wins big as Anthony Hopkins and Promising Young Woman surprise|last=Shoard|first=Catherine|date=11 April 2021|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} Shaord also opined that while the nominees were atypically diverse, including twenty-one first-time acting nominees, the eventual winners were traditional. She also asserted that ''[[Nomadland]]''{{'}}s four wins indicated it as the [[front-runner|frontrunner]] for the upcoming Academy Awards. Announced on 8 April 2021, both ceremonies had been intended to feature [[Prince William, Duke of Cambridge]], the president of BAFTA. At the opening ceremony, he planned to speak virtually with filmmakers about the [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on cinema|hardships of film production during the COVID-19 pandemic]] and would have presented a video speech about the resilience of the [[film industry]] during the 11 April ceremony.{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2021/04/prince-william-virtual-bafta-speech-musical-acts-awards-presenters-confirmed-1234730051/|title=Prince William To Make Virtual BAFTA Speech, Musical Acts & Awards Presenters Confirmed|last=Grater|first=Tom|date=8 April 2021|work=[[Deadline Hollywood]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} However, he pulled out from public engagements due to the [[Death and funeral of Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh|death]] of his grandfather, [[Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh]], on 9 April 2021.{{cite web|url=https://www.vanityfair.com/style/2021/04/prince-william-will-not-appear-at-baftas-after-death-of-prince-philip|title=Prince William Will Not Appear at BAFTAs After Death of Prince Philip|last=Hoffman|first=Jordan|date=10 April 2021|work=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} [[Chloé Zhao]] became only the second female winner for Best Director (following [[Kathryn Bigelow]] in [[63rd British Academy Film Awards|2010]]), and the first [[women of color|woman of colour]] to take the prize; she also shared [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] with four other producers. Neither [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]] winner [[Anthony Hopkins]] (for ''[[The Father (2020 film)|The Father]]'') nor [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] winner [[Frances McDormand]] (for ''Nomadland''), who also co-won Best Film, attended the ceremony and were able to be on camera when their wins were announced.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-56711121|title=Bafta Film Awards 2021: Snobbery, pianos and other takeaways|last=Smith|first=Neil|date=11 April 2021|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} At age 83, Hopkins become the oldest Best Actor winner, beating out expected posthumous nominee [[Chadwick Boseman]] (for ''[[Ma Rainey's Black Bottom (film)|Ma Rainey's Black Bottom]]''); though he was not present when his win was announced, Hopkins did deliver an acceptance speech at the press interviews following the event.{{cite web|url=https://www.insider.com/anthony-hopkins-best-actor-skipped-bafta-ceremony-the-father-2021-4|title=Anthony Hopkins beat Chadwick Boseman to win best actor, but skipped the BAFTAs to paint instead|last=Sarkisian|first=Jacob|date=12 April 2021|work=[[Business Insider|Insider]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} Hopkins' last competitive BAFTA win was twenty-seven years earlier (for ''[[The Remains of the Day (film)|The Remains of the Day]]''). Another unexpected win was first-time screenwriter and director [[Emerald Fennell]] for [[BAFTA Award for Best Original Screenplay|Best Original Screenplay]] (for ''[[Promising Young Woman]]''). Fennell accepted her award while eating a chocolate BAFTA mask. Other films with high expectations, including ''[[The Trial of the Chicago 7]]'', ''[[News of the World (film)|News of the World]]'' and ''[[Borat Subsequent Moviefilm]]'', won zero awards, while ''[[Mank]]'' only won one ([[BAFTA Award for Best Production Design|Best Production Design]]). Death was a theme among other acceptance speeches; in accepting her [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|Rising Star Award]], actress [[Bukky Bakray]] paid tribute to rapper [[DMX (rapper)|DMX]], who also died on 9 April 2021, as well as recently deceased members of her own friends and family, while [[Thomas Vinterberg]], who co-won for Best Film Not in the English Language with ''[[Another Round (film)|Another Round]]'', spoke of his daughter who died during production. South Korean actress [[Youn Yuh-jung]], who won for [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Supporting Actress]] (for ''[[Minari (film)|Minari]]''), opened her speech with an address to the [[British people]] watching, offering condolences following the death of the Duke of Edinburgh. Youn then lightened the mood by noting that she was surprised and more grateful to win due to the perception of British people as "[[snob|snobbish]]".{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/bafta-minari-yuh-jung-youn-b1829862.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210411191547/https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/bafta-minari-yuh-jung-youn-b1829862.html |archive-date=2021-04-11 |url-access=limited |url-status=live|title=Bafta Film Awards 2021: Viewers delight as Minari star Yuh-Jung Youn calls British people 'snobbish'|last=Chilton|first=Louis|date=11 April 2021|work=[[The Independent]]|access-date=12 April 2021}} The acceptance speeches of [[Daniel Kaluuya]] and Remi Weekes thanked minority voices. ==Statistics== {{col-float}} {| class="wikitable" Ronald Harwood]] * [[Kirk Douglas]] * [[Alberto Grimaldi]] * [[Larry McMurtry]] * [[Christopher Plummer]] {{div col end}} As well as appearing first in the montage, Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, royal consort and first president of BAFTA, who died the day before the opening ceremony, was paid tribute in the opening monologues on both nights of the awards.{{cite |url-access=limited |url-status=live|title=BAFTAS 2021: Dermot O'Leary and Edith Bowman Pay Tribute to Prince Philip|last=Petter|first=Olivia|date=11 April 2011|work=[[The Independent]]|access-date=12 April 2021}}{{cite web|url=https://www.swlondoner.co.uk/news/09042021-bafta-pays-tribute-to-hrh-prince-philip/|title=BAFTA pays tribute to HRH Prince Philip|last=Frost|first=Lucy|date=9 April 2021|work=South West Londoner|access-date=12 April 2021}} [[Diana Rigg|Dame Diana Rigg]] was omitted from the montage, which sparked complaints and controversy; in response, BAFTA explained that it considered her most prominent in [[Television in the United Kingdom|television]], and so she would be featured at the [[2021 British Academy Television Awards|next British Academy Television Awards ceremony]].{{cite web|url=https://metro.co.uk/2021/04/11/baftas-2021-award-show-explains-diana-riggs-absence-from-in-memoriam-14393759/|title=Baftas 2021: Award show explains why James Bond star Dame
